data.title: Nektar upgraded at Jefferies ahead of mid-stage readout for eczema drug
data.description:
Shares of Nektar Therapeutics (NASDAQ:NKTR) gained on Friday after Jefferies upgraded the stock noting an attractive risk-reward setup ahead of a mid-stage trial readout for the company’s lead asset, rezpeg targeted at atopic dermatitis (eczema).

Jefferies analyst Roger Song upgraded the company to Buy from Hold arguing that Nektar’s (NASDAQ:NKTR) negative enterprise value positions the company favorably ahead of topline data expected from a Phase 2b trial for rezpeg in eczema in Q2 2025 (potentially in June).

Following a review of early-stage trial data for the drug, Song indicated a favorable efficacy/efficacy profile for the drug, noting a novel mechanism of action compared to upcoming and approved eczema medications

“With extremely low expectations into Ph2b rezpeg AD 16W readout likely in June, we are hopeful for Rezpeg, as a novel biologic with a strong safety profile,” Song wrote as he raised his price target on NKTR to $2 from $1 per share

data.title: AI robot player Richtech Robotics gains after H.C. Wainwright points to significant upside
data.description:
Richtech Robotics (NASDAQ:RR) rallied in late Friday morning trading after H.C. Wainwright initiated coverage on the stock with a Buy rating. 

Analyst Scott Buck pointed to favorable secular tailwinds for Richtech Roboics (NASDAQ:RR), driven by improving technologies and labor shortages. He also highlighted a favorable customer return on investment following the transition to a robot-as-a-service model that is seen helping to increase adoption of service robots over the next several years.

"With RR shares down 33.7% in 2025, versus an 17.9% decline in the Russell 2000, we believe this is an attractive entry point ahead of more positive news flow and improving financial performance as the business scales," wrote Buck.

The firm assigned a price target of $3.50 to RR.

Last month, Richtech Robotics (RR) announced a strategic move to address labor shortages in the food and beverage sector through robotic automation. The company showcased the Scorpion robot's ability to serve wines and liquors while offering a sommelier-like experience. Richtech also continues to develop specialized robots such as Medbot for hospital deliveries, Titan for heavy-load transportation, and beverage-serving robots like ADAM. 

Shares of Richtech Robotics (RR) were up 4.6% at 11:20 a.m.

data.title: New York Fed's Williams reduces economic expectations
data.description:
"Monetary policy is in the right place to manage...risks as best we can," New York Fed President John Williams said on Friday.

At its March meeting, the Federal Open Market Committee kept its policy rate at 4.25%-4.50%.

"Given the uncertain effects of recently announced tariffs and other policy changes, there is an unusually wide range of outcomes that could transpire. That said, the broad contours of the outlook are becoming a bit clearer," he said according to prepared text for an event at the Puerto Rico Chamber of Commerce.

With the increased uncertainty, Williams now expects U.S. GDP growth of "somewhat below 1%." With that slowdown, he expects the unemployment rate to rise to between 4.5% and 5% over the next year.

He now expects inflation to rise to between 3.5% and 4.0%.

Given the strength of the economy at the start of the year,  "the current modestly restrictive stance of monetary policy is entirely appropriate," he said. That firm footing also gives the Federal Reserve the opportunity to assess incoming data "and ultimately positions us well to adjust to changing circumstances that affect the achievement of our dual mandate goals," he said.

Most of all, "it is critically important to keep inflation expectations well anchored as we pursue our goals of maximum employment and returning inflation to our 2 percent longer-run objective," he said.

data.title: BofA is short the S&P 500 until trade wars calm down, Fed cuts rates
data.description:
Investors should short the S&P 500 (SP500) until the Federal Reserve “cuts hard” to break the liquidation cycle, and until the U.S. and China pause their trade war and reverse global recession momentum, according to BofA.

Michael Hartnett, chief investment strategist at BofA, said the era of the U.S. “exceptionalism” is ending, and the era of a “U.S. repudiation” is starting. “It is the end of the ‘U.S. buys rest of the world goods,’ and ‘the rest of the world buys U.S. Treasuries.’”

He sees U.S. yields going higher and recommends investors to long the 2-year U.S. Treasuries (US2Y) and to short the S&P 500 (SP500) until the 4,800 level.

“We still think S&P 500 (SP500) at 4.8k is the right level to buy risk assets if policy panic makes recession short/shallow,” he said.

Higher U.S. yields, lower U.S. stocks (SP500), and a weaker U.S. dollar (DXY) are “driving global asset liquidation, will likely force policymakers to act,” he said in the note.

In addition, BofA analysts broke down how much U.S. assets foreigners own. Foreigners own 33% (or $8.5T) of U.S. Treasuries; 27% (or $4.4T) of U.S. corporate bonds; 18% (or $16.5T) of U.S. stocks.

This week, Treasuries saw the biggest weekly inflow ever, up $18.8B. Foreigners sold $6.5B of U.S. equities, and $3B of U.S. corporate bonds, and bought $0.6B of U.S. Treasuries.

data.title: Blackrock highlights 6% organic fee growth and $11.6T AUM in Q1 2025
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: BlackRock (BLK) Q1 2025

Management View

  • Chairman & CEO Laurence Fink emphasized BlackRock's robust client connectivity and strategic focus, showcasing above-target 6% organic base fee growth in Q1 2025, the strongest start since 2021, despite a volatile market environment. Total net inflows reached $140 billion, excluding episodic index equity activity.
  • CFO Martin Small highlighted $5.3 billion in Q1 revenue, a 12% year-over-year increase, driven by organic growth and higher average AUM. Operating income grew to $2 billion, and earnings per share reached $11.30, reflecting a 15% year-over-year increase.
  • BlackRock reported record AUM of $11.6 trillion and net inflows of $84 billion. Excluding episodic large low-fee institutional index redemptions, net inflows were $140 billion.
  • Private markets and ETFs were key growth drivers, with ETFs contributing $107 billion in net inflows across all categories, including $46 billion in core equity and $34 billion in fixed income ETFs.
  • Digital assets ETPs and active ETFs added $3 billion and $9 billion in net inflows, respectively.
  • BlackRock’s acquisition of Preqin boosted Q1 technology services revenue by $20 million, contributing to a total 16% year-over-year growth in this segment.
  • CFO Small noted a net investment gain of $68 million, reflecting gains in BlackRock's co-investment portfolio and a minority investment.

Outlook

  • Management reaffirmed its mid-to-high single-digit percentage increase expectation for 2025 core G&A expense, excluding HPS-related impacts.
  • BlackRock plans $375 million in share repurchases per quarter for the remainder of 2025, consistent with previous guidance.
  • Fink emphasized the continuation of secular growth trends, particularly in infrastructure and private credit, bolstered by the planned acquisition of HPS.

Financial Results

  • Net inflows of $84 billion were led by $107 billion into ETFs, with contributions from core equity, fixed income, and active ETF strategies. Private markets saw $7 billion in net inflows, led by infrastructure and private credit.
  • Base fees and securities lending revenue totaled $4.4 billion, up 16% year-over-year. Performance fees were $60 million, a decrease from the prior year due to lower private markets performance revenue.
  • Technology services revenue increased to $1.6 billion annually, with Q1 showing a 16% year-over-year rise due to sustained demand for Aladdin offerings and the Preqin acquisition.
  • Expenses increased 10% year-over-year, driven by higher asset management costs and compensation expenses linked to acquisitions and organic growth.

Q&A

  • Michael Cyprys, Morgan Stanley: Asked about client allocation trends amid market volatility. CEO Fink highlighted sustained interest in fixed income as yields rise, with no significant equity capitulation observed. Clients are increasingly interested in infrastructure and systematic equity strategies.
  • Craig Siegenthaler, Bank of America: Inquired about integrating private markets into retirement portfolios. Fink emphasized the strategic acquisition of Preqin to enhance analytics for regulatory and fiduciary standards, enabling broader adoption of private markets in retirement plans.
  • Alex Blostein, Goldman Sachs: Discussed BlackRock's long-term strategy amidst global market disruptions. Fink noted no plans for significant acquisitions, instead focusing on partnerships and organic growth, particularly in Europe and emerging markets.
  • Mike Brown, Wells Fargo: Asked about geopolitical risks to BlackRock’s positioning. Fink underscored BlackRock's localized operations in over 30 countries, emphasizing its adaptability and client-centric approach.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed interest in BlackRock's strategic initiatives in private markets, technology, and global expansion. The sentiment was slightly positive, with curiosity about long-term growth opportunities.
  • Management maintained a confident tone, highlighting resilience and adaptability amidst volatile markets. CEO Fink repeatedly assured stakeholders of BlackRock's strategic positioning.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, the tone was consistent, with a focus on growth and client relationships.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Organic base fee growth remained robust at 6%, compared to 7% annualized in Q4 2024.
  • Technology services revenue growth accelerated, supported by the Preqin acquisition.
  • Client interest in private markets and infrastructure investments continued to grow, aligning with BlackRock’s strategic acquisitions.
  • Analysts maintained consistent interest in private market integration and global expansion.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management highlighted geopolitical uncertainties and their impact on global markets but emphasized BlackRock's diversified operations and localized strategies as mitigating factors.
  • Analysts raised concerns about the integration of private markets into retail and retirement channels, given regulatory and operational challenges.

Final Takeaway

BlackRock delivered strong Q1 2025 results with record AUM, robust organic growth, and strategic advancements in private markets and technology. Management emphasized resilience and client-focused strategies, projecting confidence in its ability to navigate market uncertainties while expanding its global footprint and service offerings.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Verizon is Evercore's top wireless pick; ups rating on stock
data.description:
Evercore ISI upgraded Verizon (NYSE:VZ) to "outperform" from a previous investment rating of "in line" on Friday and named the stock its top pick under the broader wireless sector.

The research firm noted that VZ shares have outperformed the broader market meaningfully this year, even though the stock continues to lag against peers AT&T (T) and T-Mobile U.S. (TMUS).

Evercore is optimistic about Verizon’s broadband strategy across fiber and fixed wireless and added that there’s a meaningful perception gap between telecom’s fiber efforts and its peers, particularly AT&T.

They believe management has executed well towards improvements in postpaid subscriber trends at the consumer unit, showcased continued momentum across business wireless customers, and is consistently hitting its broadband subscriber targets.

They think Verizon's wireless service revenue growth should be resilient despite volatile net adds, while being cautious on postpaid phone sub growth across the industry.

"Notwithstanding some potential subscriber volatility, we remain confident in its [Verizon's] ability to meet its wireless service revenue growth target," Evercore said Friday.

VZ's price target was hiked by $4 to $48, implying an upside of nearly 12%.

data.title: Bond market shakes as the US10Y hits a two-month high
data.description:
The benchmark U.S. 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) hit a two-month high on Friday, amid a bruising bond selloff in longer-term maturities this week. 

The US10Y surged as much as 19 basis points to hit a session peak at 4.58%, its highest level since February 12. It was last up to 4.51%.

Meanwhile, the U.S. 20-year Treasury yield (US20Y) was up 10 basis points to 5.00%, and the 30-year yield (US30Y) was up 7 basis points to 4.94%.  

Traditionally, yields fall as investors seek safety by buying bonds during equity market stress. However, that traditional safe-haven role has been called into question amid this week's broader bond selloff. 

A Minneapolis Fed official in a CNBC interview said that rising yields and a falling dollar (DXY) show investors moving on from the U.S.. 

“This is not what one usually sees with regard to the dollar and rates. Damages are real. An appreciating currency amplifies tariff inflation & economic weakness,” Diane Swonk, chief economist of KPMG said. 

Many analysts attribute the trend to the unwinding of complex trading strategies like the basis trade, along with foreign investors reducing U.S. bond holdings. 

See how yields are trading across the curve here on Seeking Alpha’s bond page. 

Fixed Income Tracking ETFs: (NASDAQ:TLT), (NYSEARCA:TLH), (NASDAQ:IEF), (NASDAQ:IEI), (NASDAQ:SHY), (NYSEARCA:SGOV), (NYSEARCA:SCHO), (NYSEARCA:BIL), (NYSEARCA:AGG), (NASDAQ:BND), (NASDAQ:VCIT), (NYSEARCA:MUB), (NASDAQ:MBB), (NYSEARCA:JNK), (NYSEARCA:LQD), (NYSEARCA:HYG), and (NYSEARCA:TIP).

data.title: MarketWise reports preliminary Q1 billings of $70 million; shares jump over 13%
data.description:
• MarketWise (NASDAQ:MKTW) on Friday reported preliminary selected unaudited Q1 billings of $70 million, representing over a 26% sequential increase compared with Q4 2024.
  • Consolidated paid subscribers at March 31, 2025, were 473,000. Active free subscribers were 3 million at March 31, 2025, said the company. 
  • Cumulative dividends paid by the company over the last twelve months equates to a cash dividend yield of 16%, based on the share price as of March 31, 2025, it added.
  • As of March 31, 2025, the company is estimated to hold cash and cash equivalents of $79 million. Not included in this amount is approximately $9 million of accounts receivable, primarily related to third-party credit card providers, due to the timing of a successful March campaign where we collected the cash after quarter end. 
  • The company said it has commenced share buybacks in Q2 in accordance with the Board’s previously announced buyback authorization.
  • MarketWise plans to report full and unaudited Q1 results no later than May 15, 2025.
  • MKTW is +13.6318% to $11.42. 
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Morgan Stanley gets revenue boost from sale of X debt: report
data.description:
Elon Musk helped to boost Morgan Stanley’s (NYSE:MS) Q1 revenue, Bloomberg reported Friday, citing people with knowledge of the matter. 

The investment bank earlier in the day reported better-than-expected results for the just-ended period. It said it earned $692M in “other” revenue, up from $242M a year earlier. 

According to Bloomberg, the person said the increase was due to the sale of loans of Musk’s X social-media platform.

Morgan Stanley said in its earnings release that the gain was “principally driven by realized gains on the sale of corporate loans held-for-sale,” but it didn’t provide further details. 

The bank reported $4.3B in earnings for Q1.

Morgan Stanley led a group of banks that provided debt for Musk’s acquisition of Twitter, the predecessor of X, in 2022. The banks had not been able to find buyers for $13B of that debt. 

Musk’s xAI artificial intelligence business last month acquired X for the same amount he paid for it in 2022. Morgan Stanley was the only banker on the transaction and represented each side. 

Morgan Stanley did not immediately respond to a request for comment by Seeking Alpha.

data.title: Boeing slides as Chinese airline postpones jet delivery - Bloomberg
data.description:
Boeing (NYSE:BA) -1.8% in Friday's trading after Bloomberg reported that China's Juneyao Airlines is delaying delivery of a 787-9 Dreamliner aircraft because of President Trump's tariffs on China.

Juneyao was expected to take delivery of the widebody aircraft, valued at ~$120M, from the U.S. company in roughly three weeks but will now hold off, according to the report.

Boeing (NYSE:BA) has warned previously that the escalating trade dispute could hurt supply chains that had been severely strained since the COVID-19 pandemic and were only now showing signs of returning to normal.

For Juneyao, the postponement risks delaying its international expansion, which included plans to increase flights to Europe starting this summer; the additional routes had relied on deliveries of long-range Boeing (BA) aircraft.
